page: Amazon Web Services g6f.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, consisting of 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. For hardware acceleration, the server includes a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU providing 11 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 450 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 10 Gbps. The system operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of tested systems under stress-ng. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average read performance but a weaker write speed, which ranks in the bottom 25%. Notably, the hardware excels in LLM inference prompt processing, achieving top-tier results in the top 10% for both small and medium models, alongside strong performance for large models. Text generation speeds for these models remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the g6f.4xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile by combining dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores with fractional GPU acceleration and local NVMe storage. This configuration provides a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring GPU-assisted acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. The server is suited for lightweight machine learning inference, prompt processing, ray tracing, and graphics-intensive applications that benefit from hardware-accelerated rendering and moderate local storage capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services g6f.4xlarge is an x86_64 server built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 450 GB NVMe SSD. It incorporates a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 11 GB of VRAM to support accelerated workloads. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models, though large-block memory write speeds are relatively weak. The hardware profile offers a balanced, cost-efficient option for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration without the overhead of a full GPU. It is suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and general-purpose compute tasks requiring local NVMe storage.

tagline: An x86 server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors with fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU acceleration and local NVMe storage.

Fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 11 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
450 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
